About Me

Who I am and what I love building

Full Stack Developer & AI Enthusiast

I am a B.Tech Computer Science student from Lakireddy Bali Reddy College of Engineering, Mylavaram. Certified in AWS Cloud Practitioner, CCNA (Security & Automation), ServiceNow CSA/CAD, and Fortinet Associate, with hands-on experience across AI, cloud, cybersecurity, and enterprise platforms.

I love building intelligent, full-stack applications, leading technical communities, and solving complex real-world problems. I am a published IEEE researcher (ICMLAS-2026).

Real-Time Driver Behavior Monitoring System

Beyond the Resume

The story behind the skills — leadership, curiosity, and growth

Beyond the Classroom

Building Programs, Leading Change

"The greatest impact I created during engineering wasn't limited to software — it was enabling people, programs, and ideas to grow."

Engineering taught me how to build technology; leadership taught me how to build impact.

As Technical Initiatives Lead of the Association of Computer Geeks (ACG), I initiated and led technical workshops, coding competitions, and peer-learning programs that strengthened technical excellence and inspired continuous learning beyond the classroom.

As Organizing Committee Lead for LAKSHYA 2K24, 2K25, and 2K26, I drove end-to-end execution of large-scale technical programs, leading strategic planning, budgeting, resource optimization, logistics, and operational coordination. Through structured execution and participant-focused planning, I scaled the "Who Is KGF?" event to 300+ participants, achieving its highest participation to date.

As Student Outreach Leader for NSS and Red Cross initiatives, I led volunteer-driven outreach programs in collaboration with the Andhra Pradesh State Government and Police Department, strengthening organizational coordination, teamwork, and social impact.

These experiences shaped my ability to take initiative, lead with accountability, and transform ambitious ideas into measurable outcomes.

Engineering Under Pressure

Solving Problems Beyond the Code

"Technology is only powerful when it's applied to solve the right problem."

My engineering journey has been shaped by solving complex challenges across

Artificial Intelligence, Quantum Computing, Full-Stack Development, Cloud Computing, Cybersecurity, and Enterprise Systems.

Hackathons, internships, and real-world projects pushed me beyond implementation, demanding rapid learning, strategic decision-making, and solution-oriented thinking under pressure. Each challenge strengthened my ability to analyze requirements, evaluate trade-offs, and design scalable, reliable systems.

From developing an AI-powered Driver Behavior Monitoring System to building an Augmented Reality Shopping Platform and a Disaster Management System, every project reinforced my belief that successful engineering begins with understanding the problem before selecting the technology.

Every experience has reinforced one principle: exceptional engineers aren't defined by the technologies they know, but by their ability to solve problems, adapt quickly, and deliver meaningful solutions.

Driven by Curiosity

The Mindset Behind My Engineering Journey

"Curiosity is the force that transforms learning into innovation."

I view engineering as a continuous journey of exploration rather than a destination.

Beyond academics, I actively pursued knowledge through research, conferences, certifications, internships, and hands-on projects across Artificial Intelligence, Cloud Computing, Cybersecurity, Networking, Quantum Computing, and Software Engineering.

Every experience expanded my perspective — from understanding emerging technologies at conferences to developing practical expertise through industry certifications and research. Rather than collecting credentials, I focused on building a strong technical foundation and the ability to adapt across multiple domains.

Research taught me to question assumptions, think analytically, and embrace experimentation. Every challenge, whether successful or not, strengthened my problem-solving mindset and reinforced the importance of continuous improvement.

I believe the greatest competitive advantage an engineer can possess is the willingness to keep learning. Technologies will evolve, but curiosity, adaptability, and the drive to solve meaningful problems remain timeless qualities that define impactful engineers.
